user_query:
I’m a medical student preparing a weekly Maternal–Fetal Medicine (MFM) journal club. Please build and run a small, locally executable automation that I can schedule weekly to prepare a brief using the provided inputs and new Internet lookups.

Requirements
1) Script and schedule
- Implement a command-line script at scripts/generate_weekly_brief.py. It must run without interactive input and produce all deliverables described below in one execution.
- Create scheduler/cron_schedule.txt containing exactly one crontab line that runs the script every Sunday at 18:00 local time, assuming the project root as the working directory. Do not actually install the cron job; just provide the line. Use a command that writes outputs to the same paths specified below.

2) Local data processing and statistics
- Input: data/simulated_cases.csv (provided). For this initial run, compute statistics over ALL rows in this file (no date filtering).
- Compute per-condition counts and the mean gestational_age_weeks (rounded to 1 decimal). Also compute overall total cases and overall mean gestational_age_weeks (rounded to 1 decimal).
- Write results to output/weekly_summary.csv with columns: condition,count,mean_ga_weeks. Include one row per condition (sorted alphabetically by condition) and a final summary row with condition set to ALL, count set to the total case count, and mean_ga_weeks set to the overall mean.

3) Internet search of official guidance (essential)
- Using a public search engine, find at least one current official clinical guidance or practice resource from EACH of the following organizations/domains: ACOG (acog.org) and SMFM (smfm.org). Focus on topics related to hypertensive disorders of pregnancy or gestational diabetes.
- For each organization, capture at least one result with these fields: organization ("ACOG" or "SMFM"), query_used (the exact search string you used), result_title (page title text), result_url, and access_date in ISO format (YYYY-MM-DD).
- Write these results to output/resources/resources.json as a JSON array of objects using exactly those field names. Do not hardcode URLs in this request; actually perform the queries.

4) Update an existing local document with meeting notes and action items
- Edit docs/meeting_agenda.md by APPENDING a new section at the end with the heading: "## Weekly MFM Journal Club Summary (YYYY-MM-DD)", where YYYY-MM-DD is today’s date.
- In that section include:
  a) A brief, human-readable summary of counts per condition (you may list lines like "Hypertensive disorder: N cases").
  b) A short list titled "External resources" with one bullet per organization listing the result_title and result_url you captured in output/resources/resources.json.
  c) An "Action items" bullet list determined by these rules:
     - If Hypertensive disorder cases >= 3, include exactly this bullet: "Action: schedule mini-review of hypertensive disorders of pregnancy."
     - If Gestational diabetes cases >= 2, include exactly this bullet: "Action: review inpatient glucose management for GDM."
     - Always include exactly this bullet: "Action: assign one SMFM Consult or ACOG bulletin from resources.json for discussion."

5) Deliverables to exist after you run the script once now
- scripts/generate_weekly_brief.py (executable script)
- output/weekly_summary.csv (structured stats)
- output/resources/resources.json (structured Internet search results)
- docs/meeting_agenda.md (modified with the new summary section appended)
- scheduler/cron_schedule.txt (one valid crontab line for Sundays at 18:00)

Please implement the script, run it once in this workspace to generate the outputs above, and make sure the Internet search step is actually performed and reflected in output/resources/resources.json. Keep formatting simple and readable; focus on correctness of paths, fields, and the action item rules. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
